problem.title: Basic Calculator II
problem.description: Given a string s which represents an expression, evaluate this expression and return its value. The integer division should truncate toward zero. The expression contains non-negative integers, '+', '-', '*', '/' operators, and empty spaces. No parentheses.
assumptions: All intermediate results fit in a 32-bit integer.

example.explanation: Multiplication/division before addition/subtraction. Use a stack: process numbers with the previous operator. For * and /, pop and push result. For + and -, push +/- number. Sum the stack at the end.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Stack with Operator Precedence
solutions.description: Scan the string tracking the current number and the last seen operator (initially '+'). When a non-digit is encountered (or end of string), apply the last operator to the stack: '+' pushes the number, '-' pushes its negation, '*' and '/' pop, compute, and push back. Sum the stack for the final result.
